context: Checkpoint 2026-04-30T05:14Z after continuing atomic documentation/roadmap slices and preparing for context compaction. current_tasks: Pushed 68a7d6d3 fix(sower): declare windmill workspace dependency after reproducing the CI failure locally with npm --prefix packages/sower run build and npx turbo run build --filter=@refarm.dev/sower --cache-dir=.turbo. CI run 25148328365 passed.

Ran npm run clean:heavy --silent after heavy local builds; removed Rust target dirs for tractor, pi-agent, heartwood, and wasm validation fixture. Disk free recovered from ~2.4GB to ~7.6GB. Rust target artifact report is 0M.
Roadmap/release docs recalibrated around the user's clarified rule: Refarm should not mint/publish v0.1.0 until it is the creator's daily driver and can replace the current external pi workflow.

Local-only commits after origin/develop before this handoff: c92d2ef0 docs(release): make daily-driver gate authoritative; 078e8249 docs(index): point release planning at daily-driver gate; 7ea5987a docs(dev): require local reproduction before ci; 52bf3fea docs(release): defer publication verification gate.
docs/local-disk-hygiene.md now records the process rule: reproduce likely failures locally with the closest scoped command before using GitHub Actions as final confirmation.
docs/v0.1.0-release-gate.md no longer treats contract publication as independent of daily-driver readiness; Track B is authoritative for any v0.1.0 publication.
Final local validation for current docs-only slices passed: npm run project:validate --silent and git diff --check.
blockers: Disk remains constrained on a 99% full mount even after heavy cleanup, with about 7.6GB free. Avoid broad builds/tests. Heavy cleanup removed Rust targets, so future Rust checks will rebuild from scratch.

Use local scoped reproduction before push/CI. For dependency graph failures, npx turbo run build --filter=<changed-package> --cache-dir=.turbo is a better local approximation than package-only tsc because it includes dependencies.
Full local tractor-ts unit suite remains noisy because WASM fixtures are missing. Continue using npm run agent:streaming:clients for stream helper changes.

Broad cargo clippy --lib --tests still has unrelated await_holding_lock debt in packages/tractor/tests/pi_agent_harness.rs. npm --prefix packages/tractor run lint passes but will rebuild targets after heavy cleanup.
pi-lens/LSP continues crashing on Markdown writes with memory access out of bounds; re-read files after edits and expect auto-format warnings.
next_actions: First command after compaction: git status --branch --short; git log --oneline origin/develop..HEAD; GH_PROMPT_DISABLED=1 gh run list --repo aretw0/refarm --branch develop --limit 5; df -h /workspaces/refarm.

Decide whether to push the local docs-only ahead stack plus this handoff. Before push, local checks already passed (project validate + diff check); pre-push should be lightweight, but CI should still be treated only as confirmation.
Continue roadmap audit pragmatically: scan package ROADMAP.md files with v0.2.0+ headings and reframe only active daily-driver-critical packages, not mass-edit everything.

Potential next daily-driver slice: make a concise daily-driver readiness checklist that maps pi capabilities to Refarm/Farmhand equivalents (terminal loop, memory, todo/project board, local tools, streaming UI).
Potential technical slice after docs: choose first concrete UI consumer for StreamChunk/StreamSession reducers; keep BrowserSyncClient schema-neutral.
Run npm run clean:light --silent at checkpoint boundaries if any builds create .turbo or Rust incremental artifacts; use clean:heavy only if disk drops near 2-3GB again.

Which package roadmaps should be actively revised versus left as historical backlog labels?
Should the future v0.1 release be private/internal-only even after daily-driver readiness, or public npm publishing after explicit human override?
Which concrete web/UI consumer should first subscribe to StreamChunk/StreamSession nodes?
